headline: Freedom of Information exists in only 2 states after 14 years
context: Fourteen years after Selangor enacted Malaysia's first FOI law in 2011, only Selangor and Penang have Freedom of Information legislation, with no federal equivalent.

cards.big: Only 2 out of 13 states have FOI laws — zero at federal level after 14 years
cards.sub: Malaysia's transparency framework remains among the weakest in Southeast Asia

cards.big: OSA 1972 makes secrecy the default — no federal right to information exists
cards.sub: Citizens must prove why they need information rather than government proving why it's secret

cards.big: Selangor processed 847 FOI requests in 2025 with 78% approval rate
cards.sub: Demand proves citizens want information when given a legal avenue to request it

cards.big: Thailand, Indonesia, and Philippines all enacted national FOI laws before 2020
cards.sub: Malaysia is the only major ASEAN economy without a federal right to information

cards.big: A government that fears transparency is a government that fears its own citizens
Why has every federal government since 2008 broken its FOI enactment promise?
